pr.title: Finish match of grlib, fix fabsf declaration, fixup Fighter's x1860 naming based on usages, improved WIPs in ftCo_DamageFall and ftwaitanim
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched melee/gr/grlib.c fully enough to delete asm/melee/gr/grlib.s and switch grlib from NonMatching to Matching. Also corrected the MSL fabsf__Ff signature/implementation to use float and __fabsf, renamed Fighter dmg.x1860 to dmg.x1860_element based on repeated element usages, and cleaned WIP decomp in ftCo_DamageFall and ftwaitanim with more precise argument names, helper extraction, and a WaitStruct type.
what_changed: Deleted the 820-line asm/melee/gr/grlib.s fallback and changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/gr/grlib.c.o instead of asm/melee/gr/grlib.s.o.
Changed configure.py so melee/gr/grlib.c is listed as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Reworked grlib.c matching code around grLib_801C9EE8: renamed the local collision helper from inlineA0 to PointInsideColl, expanded temporaries for top/bottom/left/right/width/height/topn/x/y/comp, and rewrote fighter/item iteration into straightforward loops over HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters and HSD_GObj_Entities->items.
Fixed src/MSL/math.h declaration of fabsf__Ff from double fabsf__Ff(double) to float fabsf__Ff(float), and changed math_1.c implementation to return __fabsf(param_1) instead of __fabs(param_1).

Renamed Fighter::dmg.x1860 to Fighter::dmg.x1860_element in src/melee/ft/types.h and propagated the rename through damage, bury, barrel wait, shouldered, damage bind, damage ice, ft_0C31, and fighter code.

Refactored ftCo_80090594 in ftCo_DamageFall.c: argument names now describe element and ground_or_air, early returns explicitly exclude elements 10, 12, 6, and 7 and DamageIce motion, and the model shift frame calculation/use of floor normal is cleaner.
Updated ftCo_DamageFall.h to include common_structs.h and use GroundOrAir ground_or_air in the ftCo_80090594 prototype.

Improved ftwaitanim by changing ftCo_8008A6D8 anim_id type from enum_t to s32, introducing WaitStruct in ftwaitanim.h, changing ftCo_8008A7A8 to take WaitStruct*, and extracting weighted wait-animation selection into getAnimID.
smart_moves: The grlib match was completed by moving from anonymous inline-style code to a named PointInsideColl helper with explicit scalar temporaries, a common decomp tactic for controlling compiler scheduling and stack/register behavior while making intent clearer.
Switching both configure.py and obj_files.mk alongside deleting grlib.s avoided a stale asm fallback and made the matching status explicit in both build descriptions.
The fabsf__Ff fix addressed both prototype and implementation together, preserving ABI/type consistency: float input/output pairs with __fabsf rather than a double signature calling __fabs.
The x1860 rename was evidence-based: all changed uses compare or assign hit.element values or element IDs, so x1860_element gives future readers semantic guidance without changing layout.

ftwaitanim introduced a union-backed WaitStruct rather than continuing to treat the data as IntVec2, documenting that the same two-word table can be viewed as pointer-like animation IDs or integer sentinel/weights.
ftCo_DamageFall used clearer control flow with early returns instead of a dense compound condition, improving readability while keeping the same special-case element exclusions visible.
decomp_lessons: When a field is repeatedly assigned from hit.element and compared against elemental constants, renaming it to include _element is justified and improves cross-file reasoning.

For matching small geometry helpers, naming the helper after the predicate and splitting expressions into explicit f32 temporaries can be both clearer and more match-friendly than deeply nested inline arithmetic.
Build-status changes for a newly matched C file should update all relevant build metadata and remove the asm file in the same PR to prevent drift.
Library shim declarations must match the intended precision; a misdeclared float function as double can force incorrect codegen or call behavior in unrelated matching work.
Replacing placeholder vector/table types with a small local struct can make decomp intent clearer, especially for weighted animation tables using sentinels like -1.
Early-return refactors are useful when decomp WIPs contain negated compound conditions; they expose domain exceptions such as excluded damage elements and special motion states.
naming_conventions: Fighter dmg field at offset 0x1860 was renamed from x1860 to x1860_element.
ftCo_80090594 parameters were renamed from generic arg1/arg4 to element and ground_or_air.
The ftwaitanim weighted table type was named WaitStruct.
The grlib collision predicate helper was renamed PointInsideColl.
ftCo_8008A6D8 uses s32 anim_id instead of enum_t, matching the -1 sentinel check in the implementation.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted asm/melee/gr/grlib.s after the C version matched.
Changed grlib.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Changed obj_files.mk to compile src/melee/gr/grlib.c.o rather than asm/melee/gr/grlib.s.o.
Expanded arithmetic in PointInsideColl into separate f32 temporaries, likely to influence register allocation/expression ordering while preserving readable collision-bound checks.
Rewrote grLib_801C9EE8 iteration into explicit fighter and item loops, with item collision skipped for Pokemon_Random via itGetKind(cur_item) != Pokemon_Random.
Extracted ftwaitanim weighted random selection into getAnimID, preserving HSD_Randi(100) + 1, cumulative weights, OSReport, and assert behavior.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename other Fighter::dmg x18xx fields based on usage.
Search for remaining fabsf__Ff call sites affected by the float signature.
Check whether WaitStruct was later renamed or replaced with a more final wait animation table type.
Look for later grlib PRs that rename grLib_801C9EE8 or PointInsideColl to more semantic names.
Search for remaining NonMatching ground/stage library files after grlib was marked Matching.
